  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c-human-factors", "name": "Human factors in AI adoption", "aliases": ["people issues", "human strategy"], "description": "The people-side, organizational, and cultural determinants that the document argues are the dominant cause of AI adoption failure.", "grounding_span": "63% of AI implementation challenges stem from human factors, not technical limitations.", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-technical-limitations", "name": "Technical implementation issues", "aliases": ["technical limitations", "technology problems"], "description": "Technology-side causes of AI failure, which the document frames as a minority of challenges.", "grounding_span": "Technical implementation issues account for only 16% of AI challenges.", "location": "Why Do AI Projects Fail? The Human Factors Nobody Wants to Discuss"},
    {"id": "c-ai-adoption", "name": "AI adoption", "aliases": ["AI implementation", "AI transformation"], "description": "The organizational process of putting AI into productive, sustained use.", "grounding_span": "fundamentally alter how every organization approaches AI adoption", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-change-management", "name": "Change management", "aliases": ["change management circles"], "description": "The discipline of managing organizational change, positioned as the correct frame for AI adoption.", "grounding_span": "There's a statistic making the rounds in change management circles", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-employee-resistance", "name": "Employee resistance", "aliases": ["resistance to AI"], "description": "The label executives apply to non-adoption, which the document reframes as fear.", "grounding_span": "What executives dismissively label \"employee resistance\"", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"id": "c-existential-fear", "name": "Existential fear of replacement", "aliases": ["fear", "fear of job loss"], "description": "Employees' fundamental fear that AI will replace them or make their skills obsolete.", "grounding_span": "usually something far more fundamental: existential fear.", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"id": "c-quiet-non-adoption", "name": "Quiet non-adoption and workarounds", "aliases": ["workarounds", "quiet non-adoption"], "description": "Covert avoidance behaviors where employees appear to adopt while circumventing the system.", "grounding_span": "Instead, they engage in quiet non-adoption. They find workarounds.", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"id": "c-insufficient-training", "name": "Insufficient training", "aliases": ["training gap"], "description": "A named adoption challenge, though the document argues fear underlies it.", "grounding_span": "38% of AI adoption challenges stem from insufficient training", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"id": "c-change-saturation", "name": "Change saturation and fatigue", "aliases": ["change fatigue", "change saturation point"], "description": "The cumulative exhaustion from too many organizational initiatives, degrading capacity to absorb AI.", "grounding_span": "75% of organizations report they are either nearing, at, or past the change saturation point.", "location": "Change Saturation and Fatigue"},
    {"id": "c-change-capacity", "name": "Change capacity", "aliases": ["change capacity limit"], "description": "The finite amount of change an organization's people can absorb before defensive behaviors emerge.", "grounding_span": "When employees hit their change capacity limit", "location": "Change Saturation and Fatigue"},
    {"id": "c-ceo-oversight-ai-governance", "name": "CEO oversight of AI governance", "aliases": ["CEO ownership of AI"], "description": "Direct CEO ownership of AI governance, presented as the element most correlated with bottom-line impact.", "grounding_span": "CEO oversight of AI governance is the single element most correlated with bottom-line impact from AI initiatives.", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "c-ai-governance", "name": "AI governance", "aliases": ["governance of AI"], "description": "The structures and decisions by which an organization directs and controls AI.", "grounding_span": "When CEOs directly own AI governance—not just sponsor it, but own it", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "c-board-engagement", "name": "Board engagement with AI", "aliases": ["board oversight"], "description": "The degree to which boards receive AI metrics and engage substantively with AI strategy.", "grounding_span": "When boards engage substantively with AI strategy, outcomes improve measurably.", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "c-pilot-purgatory", "name": "Pilot purgatory", "aliases": ["pilot stage trap"], "description": "The condition of being stuck in endless proofs-of-concept that never scale to enterprise adoption.", "grounding_span": "trapped in what researchers call \"pilot purgatory\"", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"id": "c-scaling", "name": "Scaling AI across the enterprise", "aliases": ["enterprise adoption", "scaling"], "description": "Moving AI beyond pilots to broad organizational deployment.", "grounding_span": "having not begun scaling AI across the enterprise despite years of experimentation", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"id": "c-org-fragmentation", "name": "Organizational fragmentation", "aliases": ["silos", "organizational silos"], "description": "AI initiatives dying in the gaps between functional silos because nobody owns the integrated whole.", "grounding_span": "AI initiatives routinely die in the gaps between organizational silos.", "location": "Organizational Fragmentation"},
    {"id": "c-cross-functional-support", "name": "Cross-functional support", "aliases": ["cross-functional coordination"], "description": "Coordination across functions, whose absence keeps companies stuck in pilot mode.", "grounding_span": "lack of cross-functional support", "location": "Organizational Fragmentation"},
    {"id": "c-ai-as-tech-project", "name": "Treating AI as a technology project", "aliases": ["technology initiative framing"], "description": "The fundamental error of categorizing AI adoption as a technology rather than organizational initiative.", "grounding_span": "The most fundamental error is categorizing AI adoption as a technology initiative.", "location": "Treating AI as a Technology Project"},
    {"id": "c-speed-over-readiness", "name": "Optimizing for speed over readiness", "aliases": ["executive impatience"], "description": "Prioritizing rapid deployment over the foundational preparation adoption requires.", "grounding_span": "Speed and readiness exist in tension.", "location": "Optimizing for Speed Over Readiness"},
    {"id": "c-org-readiness", "name": "Organizational readiness", "aliases": ["readiness"], "description": "An organization's preparedness across multiple dimensions to absorb AI.", "grounding_span": "assess your organizational readiness across five dimensions", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-two-way-communication", "name": "Two-way communication and engagement", "aliases": ["genuine dialogue", "engaging with"], "description": "Genuine bidirectional dialogue that succeeds where one-directional communication fails.", "grounding_span": "The organizations that succeed at AI adoption create genuine two-way communication.", "location": "Communicating at Instead of Engaging With"},
    {"id": "c-social-norms", "name": "Social norms of adoption", "aliases": ["social context", "shifting social norms"], "description": "The team-level norms that shape whether individuals adopt AI.", "grounding_span": "You have to shift social norms.", "location": "Ignoring the Social Dynamics of Adoption"},
    {"id": "c-social-proof", "name": "Social proof and peer influence", "aliases": ["peer influence"], "description": "Adoption spread through visible peer usage rather than individual persuasion.", "grounding_span": "it has to create the conditions for social proof and peer influence.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "c-elevation-not-efficiency", "name": "Reframing AI as elevation rather than efficiency", "aliases": ["elevation not elimination"], "description": "Positioning AI as freeing people for higher-value work rather than cutting headcount.", "grounding_span": "When you introduce AI as a way to eliminate the tedious parts of work so people can focus on what they do best", "location": "Reframe AI as Elevation, Not Efficiency"},
    {"id": "c-psychological-safety", "name": "Psychological safety", "aliases": ["safety to express concerns"], "description": "An environment where employees can voice concerns without being labeled resistant.", "grounding_span": "creating genuine psychological safety for employees to express concerns", "location": "Address Fear Before Functionality"},
    {"id": "c-experimentation-culture", "name": "Experimentation culture", "aliases": ["safe experimentation"], "description": "A culture that actively encourages trying AI and learning from mistakes, named the single most significant success factor.", "grounding_span": "they actively encourage employees to experiment with AI capabilities.", "location": "Create Psychological Safety for Experimentation"},
    {"id": "c-champion-model", "name": "Structured champion model", "aliases": ["champion network"], "description": "A multi-level model creating AI advocates to drive social adoption.", "grounding_span": "The most effective approach is a structured champion model that creates AI advocates at multiple levels", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "c-executive-champions", "name": "Executive Champions", "aliases": [], "description": "Senior leaders who visibly use AI themselves rather than only sponsoring it.", "grounding_span": "Senior leaders who don't just sponsor AI initiatives but visibly use AI themselves.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "c-operational-champions", "name": "Operational Champions", "aliases": [], "description": "Middle managers and team leads who translate strategy into daily practice.", "grounding_span": "Middle managers and team leads who translate organizational strategy into daily practice.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "c-power-users", "name": "Power Users", "aliases": [], "description": "Peer-level experts who provide on-the-ground support and normalize AI usage.", "grounding_span": "Peer-level experts who provide on-the-ground support and make AI usage normal within their teams.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "c-comprehensive-vs-incremental", "name": "Comprehensive vs incremental initiatives", "aliases": ["go comprehensive"], "description": "The counterintuitive finding that larger, comprehensive initiatives adopt more smoothly than incremental ones.", "grounding_span": "larger, more comprehensive AI initiatives tend to go smoother than smaller, incremental ones.", "location": "Go Comprehensive, Not Incremental"},
    {"id": "c-workflow-redesign", "name": "Workflow redesign", "aliases": ["redesign vs overlay"], "description": "Rethinking workflows from scratch with AI in mind rather than layering AI onto existing processes.", "grounding_span": "Organizations that capture real value rethink workflows from scratch with AI capabilities in mind.", "location": "Workflow Redesign, Not Overlay"},
    {"id": "c-transformative-ambition", "name": "Transformative ambition", "aliases": ["growth and innovation"], "description": "Pursuing growth and innovation rather than only efficiency, characteristic of high performers.", "grounding_span": "high performers are significantly more likely to also pursue growth and innovation.", "location": "Transformative Ambition, Not Just Efficiency"},
    {"id": "c-efficiency", "name": "Efficiency as an AI objective", "aliases": ["cost reduction"], "description": "The most commonly cited AI objective, which employees perceive as threat.", "grounding_span": "While 80% of all organizations cite efficiency as their AI objective", "location": "Transformative Ambition, Not Just Efficiency"},
    {"id": "c-integration-across-functions", "name": "Integration of AI strategy across functions", "aliases": ["cross-functional integration"], "description": "Distributing AI strategy across operations, HR, finance, and business units rather than isolating it in IT.", "grounding_span": "High performers integrate AI strategy across organizational functions rather than isolating it in IT or a dedicated AI team.", "location": "Integration Across Functions"},
    {"id": "c-high-performers", "name": "High performers", "aliases": ["high-performing organizations"], "description": "The minority of organizations seeing significant bottom-line impact from AI.", "grounding_span": "the 6% of high-performing organizations", "location": "What Successful AI Adoption Actually Looks Like"},
    {"id": "c-cognitive-overload", "name": "Cognitive overload from monitoring AI", "aliases": ["AI brain fry"], "description": "Overload experienced by workers monitoring AI outputs, linked to more errors and higher intent to quit.", "grounding_span": "14% of AI-using workers now experience cognitive overload", "location": "Why This Matters Now"},
    {"id": "c-mid-market-companies", "name": "Mid-market companies", "aliases": [], "description": "Organizations of 50 to 5,000 employees, the document's target audience.", "grounding_span": "Based on our work with mid-market companies—organizations with 50 to 5,000 employees", "location": "A Framework for Human-Centered AI Adoption"},
    {"id": "c-humans-first", "name": "Humans First methodology", "aliases": ["Humans First approach"], "description": "bosio.digital's methodology combining digital transformation experience with change expertise.", "grounding_span": "Our \"Humans First\" methodology combines 25+ years of digital transformation experience", "location": "Why This Matters Now"},
    {"id": "c-phase1", "name": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement", "aliases": [], "description": "Assess organizational readiness across five dimensions before selecting technology.", "grounding_span": "Understand Before You Implement", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-phase2", "name": "Phase 2: Discover Use Cases Through Collaboration", "aliases": [], "description": "Surface use cases through cross-functional collaboration with the people doing the work.", "grounding_span": "Discover Use Cases Through Collaboration", "location": "Phase 2: Discover Use Cases Through Collaboration"},
    {"id": "c-phase3", "name": "Phase 3: Build Change Capacity Alongside Technology", "aliases": [], "description": "Integrate change-management milestones with technology milestones.", "grounding_span": "Build Change Capacity Alongside Technology", "location": "Phase 3: Build Change Capacity Alongside Technology"},
    {"id": "c-phase4", "name": "Phase 4: Design Workflows That Elevate", "aliases": [], "description": "Design workflows so AI makes work more meaningful for humans.", "grounding_span": "Design Workflows That Elevate", "location": "Phase 4: Design Workflows That Elevate"},
    {"id": "c-dim-strategy-vision", "name": "Strategy & Vision", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness dimension: alignment on why AI matters for the specific organization.", "grounding_span": "Is there genuine alignment on why AI matters for your organization?", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-dim-data-infrastructure", "name": "Data & Infrastructure", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness dimension: whether data can feed the planned systems.", "grounding_span": "Can you actually feed the systems you're planning to implement?", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-dim-technical-capabilities", "name": "Technical Capabilities", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness dimension: skills to implement and maintain AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Do you have the skills to implement and maintain AI systems?", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-dim-people-culture", "name": "People & Culture", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness dimension: appetite for change and remaining change capacity.", "grounding_span": "What's the real appetite for change in your organization?", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-dim-governance-ethics", "name": "Governance & Ethics", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness dimension: responsible decision-making and oversight structures for AI.", "grounding_span": "How will you make decisions about AI responsibly?", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"id": "c-team-tech", "name": "Tech (TEAM dimension)", "aliases": ["Technical"], "description": "Technology dimension of the TEAM readiness framework.", "grounding_span": "Technical Data Skills Process Culture", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-team-data", "name": "Data (TEAM dimension)", "aliases": [], "description": "Data dimension of the TEAM readiness framework.", "grounding_span": "Technical Data Skills Process Culture", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-team-skills", "name": "Skills (TEAM dimension)", "aliases": [], "description": "Skills dimension of the TEAM readiness framework.", "grounding_span": "Technical Data Skills Process Culture", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-team-process", "name": "Process (TEAM dimension)", "aliases": [], "description": "Process dimension of the TEAM readiness framework.", "grounding_span": "Technical Data Skills Process Culture", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "c-team-culture", "name": "Culture (TEAM dimension)", "aliases": [], "description": "Culture dimension of the TEAM readiness framework.", "grounding_span": "Technical Data Skills Process Culture", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d-pilot-purgatory", "term": "pilot purgatory", "verbatim_text": "an endless cycle of proofs-of-concept that impress in demos but never achieve enterprise adoption", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "\"pilot purgatory\"—an endless cycle of proofs-of-concept that impress in demos but never achieve enterprise adoption.", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"id": "d-executive-champions", "term": "Executive Champions", "verbatim_text": "Senior leaders who don't just sponsor AI initiatives but visibly use AI themselves.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Senior leaders who don't just sponsor AI initiatives but visibly use AI themselves.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "d-operational-champions", "term": "Operational Champions", "verbatim_text": "Middle managers and team leads who translate organizational strategy into daily practice.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Middle managers and team leads who translate organizational strategy into daily practice.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"id": "d-power-users", "term": "Power Users", "verbatim_text": "Peer-level experts who provide on-the-ground support and make AI usage normal within their teams.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "Peer-level experts who provide on-the-ground support and make AI usage normal within their teams.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl-63-human-factors", "claim_text": "63% of AI implementation challenges stem from human factors, not technical limitations.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "63% of AI implementation challenges stem from human factors, not technical limitations.", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "cl-prosci-sample", "claim_text": "The 63% finding comes from Prosci research studying 1,107 professionals across industries.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "This finding comes from Prosci's comprehensive research studying 1,107 professionals across industries", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "cl-invest-two-to-one", "claim_text": "For every dollar spent on AI technology, organizations should spend two on preparing their people.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "For every dollar organizations spend on AI technology, they should probably be spending two on preparing their people.", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"id": "cl-48-production", "claim_text": "Only 48% of AI projects make it into production, per Gartner.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Only 48% of AI projects make it into production", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"id": "cl-30-genai-abandoned", "claim_text": "30% of generative AI projects will be abandoned after proof of concept by end of 2025.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "30% of generative AI projects will be abandoned after proof of concept by end of 2025", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"id": "cl-42-abandoned", "claim_text": "42% of companies abandoned most of their AI initiatives in 2025, up from 17% in 2024.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "42% of companies abandoned most of their AI initiatives in 2025", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"id": "cl-88-use-ai", "claim_text": "88% of organizations now use AI in at least one business function.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "88% of organizations now use AI in at least one business function", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"id": "cl-6-high-performers", "claim_text": "Only 6% of organizations qualify as high performers seeing significant bottom-line impact from AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "6% qualify as \"high performers\"", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"id": "cl-70-30", "claim_text": "Success is 70% people and ways of working, and only 30% technology.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "\"Success is 70% people and ways of working, and only 30% technology.\"", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"id": "cl-16-technical", "claim_text": "Technical implementation issues account for only 16% of AI challenges.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Technical implementation issues account for only 16% of AI challenges.", "location": "Why Do AI Projects Fail? The Human Factors Nobody Wants to Discuss"},
    {"id": "cl-38-training", "claim_text": "38% of AI adoption challenges stem from insufficient training.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "38% of AI adoption challenges stem from insufficient training", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"id": "cl-75-saturation", "claim_text": "75% of organizations report they are nearing, at, or past the change saturation point.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "75% of organizations report they are either nearing, at, or past the change saturation point.", "location": "Change Saturation and Fatigue"},
    {"id": "cl-ceo-oversight-correlated", "claim_text": "CEO oversight of AI governance is the single element most correlated with bottom-line impact from AI initiatives.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "CEO oversight of AI governance is the single element most correlated with bottom-line impact from AI initiatives.", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "cl-28-ceo-involvement", "claim_text": "Only 28% of organizations have direct CEO involvement in AI governance.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "have direct CEO involvement in AI governance", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "cl-15-boards-metrics", "claim_text": "Only 15% of boards receive AI-related metrics.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "receive AI-related metrics", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "cl-66-directors-knowledge", "claim_text": "66% of board directors report having limited to no knowledge or experience with AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "report having \"limited to no knowledge or experience\" with AI", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "cl-directors-not-on-agenda", "claim_text": "Nearly one in three directors say AI doesn't even appear on their board agendas.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "say AI doesn't even appear on their board agendas", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"id": "cl-two-thirds-pilot", "claim_text": "Nearly two-thirds of organizations remain stuck in the pilot stage.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "nearly two-thirds of organizations remain stuck in the pilot stage", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"id": "cl-37-coworkers", "claim_text": "37% of employees don't use AI even when they can, simply because their coworkers aren't using it.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "37% of employees don't use AI even when they can, simply because their coworkers aren't using it.", "location": "Ignoring the Social Dynamics of Adoption"},
    {"id": "cl-comprehensive-smoother", "claim_text": "Larger, more comprehensive AI initiatives tend to go smoother than smaller, incremental ones.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "larger, more comprehensive AI initiatives tend to go smoother than smaller, incremental ones.", "location": "Go Comprehensive, Not Incremental"},
    {"id": "cl-experimentation-factor", "claim_text": "Experimentation culture is the single most significant factor distinguishing AI adoption success from failure.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "This difference in experimentation culture represents", "location": "Create Psychological Safety for Experimentation"},
    {"id": "cl-3x-ownership", "claim_text": "High performers are three times more likely to have senior leaders demonstrating genuine ownership of AI initiatives.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "than their peers to have senior leaders who demonstrate genuine ownership of AI initiatives.", "location": "Visible, Engaged Leadership"},
    {"id": "cl-100-advanced-leadership", "claim_text": "100% of organizations at the advanced stage of AI adoption had leadership that clearly communicated AI's importance and fostered collaboration.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "had leadership that clearly communicated AI's importance and fostered collaboration.", "location": "Visible, Engaged Leadership"},
    {"id": "cl-workflow-redesign-factor", "claim_text": "The single biggest factor affecting financial impact from AI is whether organizations redesign workflows rather than the technology itself.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The single biggest factor affecting financial impact from AI isn't the technology itself", "location": "Workflow Redesign, Not Overlay"},
    {"id": "cl-80-efficiency", "claim_text": "80% of all organizations cite efficiency as their AI objective.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "While 80% of all organizations cite efficiency as their AI objective", "location": "Transformative Ambition, Not Just Efficiency"},
    {"id": "cl-14-cognitive-overload", "claim_text": "14% of AI-using workers experience cognitive overload from monitoring AI outputs.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "14% of AI-using workers now experience cognitive overload", "location": "Why This Matters Now"},
    {"id": "cl-human-transformation", "claim_text": "AI transformation is fundamentally a human transformation.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "AI transformation is fundamentally a human transformation.", "location": "Why This Matters Now"}
  ],

    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-human-factors", "grounding_span": "63% of AI implementation challenges stem from human factors, not technical limitations.", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-technical-limitations", "grounding_span": "Technical implementation issues account for only 16% of AI challenges.", "location": "Why Do AI Projects Fail? The Human Factors Nobody Wants to Discuss"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-ai-adoption", "grounding_span": "fundamentally alter how every organization approaches AI adoption", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-change-management", "grounding_span": "There's a statistic making the rounds in change management circles", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-employee-resistance", "grounding_span": "What executives dismissively label \"employee resistance\"", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-existential-fear", "grounding_span": "usually something far more fundamental: existential fear.", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-quiet-non-adoption", "grounding_span": "Instead, they engage in quiet non-adoption. They find workarounds.", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-insufficient-training", "grounding_span": "38% of AI adoption challenges stem from insufficient training", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-change-saturation", "grounding_span": "75% of organizations report they are either nearing, at, or past the change saturation point.", "location": "Change Saturation and Fatigue"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-ceo-oversight-ai-governance", "grounding_span": "CEO oversight of AI governance is the single element most correlated with bottom-line impact from AI initiatives.", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-ai-governance", "grounding_span": "When CEOs directly own AI governance—not just sponsor it, but own it", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-board-engagement", "grounding_span": "When boards engage substantively with AI strategy, outcomes improve measurably.", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-pilot-purgatory", "grounding_span": "trapped in what researchers call \"pilot purgatory\"", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-org-fragmentation", "grounding_span": "AI initiatives routinely die in the gaps between organizational silos.", "location": "Organizational Fragmentation"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-ai-as-tech-project", "grounding_span": "The most fundamental error is categorizing AI adoption as a technology initiative.", "location": "Treating AI as a Technology Project"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-speed-over-readiness", "grounding_span": "Speed and readiness exist in tension.", "location": "Optimizing for Speed Over Readiness"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-org-readiness", "grounding_span": "assess your organizational readiness across five dimensions", "location": "Phase 1: Understand Before You Implement"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-two-way-communication", "grounding_span": "The organizations that succeed at AI adoption create genuine two-way communication.", "location": "Communicating at Instead of Engaging With"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-social-norms", "grounding_span": "You have to shift social norms.", "location": "Ignoring the Social Dynamics of Adoption"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-social-proof", "grounding_span": "it has to create the conditions for social proof and peer influence.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-elevation-not-efficiency", "grounding_span": "When you introduce AI as a way to eliminate the tedious parts of work so people can focus on what they do best", "location": "Reframe AI as Elevation, Not Efficiency"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-psychological-safety", "grounding_span": "creating genuine psychological safety for employees to express concerns", "location": "Address Fear Before Functionality"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-experimentation-culture", "grounding_span": "they actively encourage employees to experiment with AI capabilities.", "location": "Create Psychological Safety for Experimentation"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-champion-model", "grounding_span": "The most effective approach is a structured champion model that creates AI advocates at multiple levels", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-comprehensive-vs-incremental", "grounding_span": "larger, more comprehensive AI initiatives tend to go smoother than smaller, incremental ones.", "location": "Go Comprehensive, Not Incremental"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-workflow-redesign", "grounding_span": "Organizations that capture real value rethink workflows from scratch with AI capabilities in mind.", "location": "Workflow Redesign, Not Overlay"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-transformative-ambition", "grounding_span": "high performers are significantly more likely to also pursue growth and innovation.", "location": "Transformative Ambition, Not Just Efficiency"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-efficiency", "grounding_span": "While 80% of all organizations cite efficiency as their AI objective", "location": "Transformative Ambition, Not Just Efficiency"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-integration-across-functions", "grounding_span": "High performers integrate AI strategy across organizational functions rather than isolating it in IT or a dedicated AI team.", "location": "Integration Across Functions"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-high-performers", "grounding_span": "the 6% of high-performing organizations", "location": "What Successful AI Adoption Actually Looks Like"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-cognitive-overload", "grounding_span": "14% of AI-using workers now experience cognitive overload", "location": "Why This Matters Now"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-mid-market-companies", "grounding_span": "Based on our work with mid-market companies—organizations with 50 to 5,000 employees", "location": "A Framework for Human-Centered AI Adoption"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "c-humans-first", "grounding_span": "Our \"Humans First\" methodology combines 25+ years of digital transformation experience", "location": "Why This Matters Now"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "d-pilot-purgatory", "grounding_span": "\"pilot purgatory\"—an endless cycle of proofs-of-concept that impress in demos but never achieve enterprise adoption.", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "d-executive-champions", "grounding_span": "Senior leaders who don't just sponsor AI initiatives but visibly use AI themselves.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "d-operational-champions", "grounding_span": "Middle managers and team leads who translate organizational strategy into daily practice.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "d-power-users", "grounding_span": "Peer-level experts who provide on-the-ground support and make AI usage normal within their teams.", "location": "Build Champions, Not Just Users"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-63-human-factors", "grounding_span": "63% of AI implementation challenges stem from human factors, not technical limitations.", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-prosci-sample", "grounding_span": "This finding comes from Prosci's comprehensive research studying 1,107 professionals across industries", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-invest-two-to-one", "grounding_span": "For every dollar organizations spend on AI technology, they should probably be spending two on preparing their people.", "location": "The Number That Should Reshape Your AI Strategy"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-48-production", "grounding_span": "Only 48% of AI projects make it into production", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-30-genai-abandoned", "grounding_span": "30% of generative AI projects will be abandoned after proof of concept by end of 2025", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-42-abandoned", "grounding_span": "42% of companies abandoned most of their AI initiatives in 2025", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-88-use-ai", "grounding_span": "88% of organizations now use AI in at least one business function", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-6-high-performers", "grounding_span": "6% qualify as \"high performers\"", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-70-30", "grounding_span": "\"Success is 70% people and ways of working, and only 30% technology.\"", "location": "The Failure Rates Are Getting Worse, Not Better"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-16-technical", "grounding_span": "Technical implementation issues account for only 16% of AI challenges.", "location": "Why Do AI Projects Fail? The Human Factors Nobody Wants to Discuss"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-38-training", "grounding_span": "38% of AI adoption challenges stem from insufficient training", "location": "Fear, Not Resistance"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-75-saturation", "grounding_span": "75% of organizations report they are either nearing, at, or past the change saturation point.", "location": "Change Saturation and Fatigue"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-ceo-oversight-correlated", "grounding_span": "CEO oversight of AI governance is the single element most correlated with bottom-line impact from AI initiatives.", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-28-ceo-involvement", "grounding_span": "have direct CEO involvement in AI governance", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-15-boards-metrics", "grounding_span": "receive AI-related metrics", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-66-directors-knowledge", "grounding_span": "report having \"limited to no knowledge or experience\" with AI", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-directors-not-on-agenda", "grounding_span": "say AI doesn't even appear on their board agendas", "location": "The Leadership Vacuum"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-two-thirds-pilot", "grounding_span": "nearly two-thirds of organizations remain stuck in the pilot stage", "location": "Pilot Purgatory"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-37-coworkers", "grounding_span": "37% of employees don't use AI even when they can, simply because their coworkers aren't using it.", "location": "Ignoring the Social Dynamics of Adoption"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-comprehensive-smoother", "grounding_span": "larger, more comprehensive AI initiatives tend to go smoother than smaller, incremental ones.", "location": "Go Comprehensive, Not Incremental"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-experimentation-factor", "grounding_span": "This difference in experimentation culture represents", "location": "Create Psychological Safety for Experimentation"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-3x-ownership", "grounding_span": "than their peers to have senior leaders who demonstrate genuine ownership of AI initiatives.", "location": "Visible, Engaged Leadership"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "why-ai-projects-fail-the-63-human-factor-problem", "to_id": "cl-100-advanced-leadership", "grounding_span": "had leadership that clearly communicated AI's importance and fostered collaboration.", "location": "Visible, Engaged Leadership"},

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "training without addressing underlying fears is futile", "suggested_edge": "ineffective_without", "from_id": "c-insufficient-training", "to_id": "c-existential-fear", "note": "Training is a solution that fails unless existential fear is addressed first; no edge type captures conditional ineffectiveness."},
    {"grounding_span": "The response to this fear is often subtle but devastating to AI initiatives.", "suggested_edge": "causes", "from_id": "c-existential-fear", "to_id": "c-quiet-non-adoption", "note": "Existential fear causally produces quiet non-adoption; schema has no causal edge."},
    {"grounding_span": "they develop defensive behaviors.", "suggested_edge": "causes", "from_id": "c-change-saturation", "to_id": "c-quiet-non-adoption", "note": "Change saturation causes defensive/status-quo behaviors."},
    {"grounding_span": "CEO oversight of AI governance is the single element most correlated with bottom-line impact from AI initiatives.", "suggested_edge": "correlated_with", "from_id": "c-ceo-oversight-ai-governance", "to_id": "c-high-performers", "note": "A correlational (predictive) relationship to bottom-line impact; schema has no correlated_with edge."},
    {"grounding_span": "Organizations that capture real value rethink workflows from scratch with AI capabilities in mind.", "suggested_edge": "drives", "from_id": "c-workflow-redesign", "to_id": "c-high-performers", "note": "Workflow redesign is presented as the biggest driver of financial impact/value capture."},
    {"grounding_span": "It makes AI adoption feel like an upgrade rather than a threat.", "suggested_edge": "mitigates", "from_id": "c-elevation-not-efficiency", "to_id": "c-employee-resistance", "note": "Reframing AI as elevation reduces resistance/fear; no mitigation edge exists."},
    {"grounding_span": "it transforms how that training lands.", "suggested_edge": "enables", "from_id": "c-champion-model", "to_id": "c-social-proof", "note": "The champion network enables social proof that makes training effective."},
    {"grounding_span": "Technical Data Skills Process Culture", "suggested_edge": "based_on", "from_id": "i-ai-readiness-assessment", "to_id": "f-team", "note": "The AI Readiness Assessment instrument is structured on the TEAM framework; schema has no Instrument→Framework edge."}
  ]
}
